text: I am a programmer with asperations to become a great game dev.
I mainly code in Javascript, although I do every now and then use Python or Godot.

I stared programming in 6th grade, during Covid, on Khan academy. I kinda just wanted to learn how, not for really any reason at the time.  Over the next couple years, I became pretty fluent programming on Khan academy, but I felt limited by the envirment, so I started learning a few different languages, like python, C#, godot, etc. Eventrally, I ended up settling back to JS, as it's just the language I was most confortable with. But that time was different, as I was fully working in a browser rather then a sandboxed learning platform.   Then my skill started to majorly grow. Over the next few years in highschool, I learend content creation on youtube, godot, python/pygame, how to use firebase and make multiplayer/online services. How to make advanced data structure, etc. And that brings us to today. I am mainly doing game development, with a side of software & game asset creation.
Having just a website is boring. I'm a game developer, after all. So I made the background a game.
So, I present Shoot The Stars: An incremental game about destrying the universe, one star at a time.
Start by clicking on of the stars in the background to gain star fragments.
